summary: Polymarket SDK typosquat exfiltrates wallets, SSH/AWS credentials, and seed phrases on install
details: The package impersonates the Polymarket SDK under an unrelated scope and ships only a credential-stealing script wired to both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks. On `npm install`, postinstall.js recursively walks user home directories (Desktop, Documents, Downloads, OneDrive, /root, /tmp) and collects: crypto wallet files (wallet.json, keystore.json, id.json for Solana, metamask.json, phantom.json, mnemonic.txt, seed.txt, private.key), SSH private keys (id_rsa, id_ecdsa, id_ed25519, deploy keys), ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials,.env files, and Chrome/Brave/Edge extension storage for MetaMask (extension id nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn) and Phantom. It additionally scans.txt/.md/.rtf/.csv files for BIP39 mnemonic word matches and filename keywords in English and Spanish (seed, mnemonic, semilla, frase, recuperacion, wallet, private, secret, passphrase, metamask, phantom, keystore, trezor, ledger). Collected data is POSTed in cleartext HTTP to hardcoded C2 at http://107.161.90.180:7777. There is no legitimate SDK code in the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
